explicit_text: My great-great-great grandpa Ludwig Draeger was the first in our family to come to America from Germany with a dream of farming. In 1883, he bought a farm in Penn Township, Brownton, Minnesota, and worked hard to build a legacy. He purchased land around the farm to create mini farms for his children, raising dairy cows and pigs. Ludwig's determination and work ethic laid the foundation for our family's values and the farm's continued existence.

His determination and work ethic laid the foundation for our family's values and the farm's continued existence.

explicit_text: The farm that has been in my family since 1883 is located in Penn Township, Minnesota. This place is where my ancestor Ludwig Draeger settled and built the farm that has been passed down through generations. Penn Township represents the roots of our family’s hard work and farming tradition, and it’s where the land was divided into mini farms for Ludwig’s children. Even today, the farm remains a significant part of our family history tied to this community.

Even today, the farm remains a significant part of our family history tied to this community.

explicit_text: Our family farm is situated in Brownton, Minnesota, within Penn Township. Brownton is the local community that has surrounded our farm for over a century. The farm has been a source of livelihood and pride for my family, and even now it is rented out to a local farmer from Brownton, keeping our family name connected to the area. This place holds many memories for my grandpa, who grew up there and lived the farming life firsthand.

The farm has been a source of livelihood and pride for my family, and even now it is rented out to a local farmer from this town, keeping our family name connected to the area. This place holds many memories for my grandpa, who grew up there and lived the farming life firsthand.

explicit_text: After Ludwig Draeger passed away, ownership of the farm was passed down to his son, Robert Draeger. Robert lived on the farm with his family and continued the tradition of hard work that Ludwig started. He played an important role in maintaining the farm as a family asset and passing it on to the next generation. Robert's stewardship helped keep the farm alive through the early 20th century.
implicit_text: After my great-great-great grandpa passed away, ownership of the farm was passed down to his son. He played an important role in maintaining the farm as a family asset and passing it on to the next generation. His stewardship helped keep the farm alive through the early 20th century.

explicit_text: My great-grandpa Charles Draeger was the last family member to own the farm before it was rented out. In 1976, he hosted a big auction on the farm that attracted people from all over Minnesota, selling equipment and animals. Charles was recognized for his dedication when, in 1983, he received an award from Rudy Perpich, the Governor of Minnesota, honoring over 100 years of family service and contributions. He modernized the farm with new equipment but kept the main building intact, preserving our family heritage.

explicit_text: Rudy Perpich was the Governor of Minnesota who, in 1983, awarded my great-grandpa Charles Draeger for the family farm’s century-long contributions. The letter from Governor Perpich praised our family for their service to the community, state, nation, and even the world. His recognition meant a lot to us because it acknowledged the hard work and dedication that started with Ludwig Draeger and continued through generations.

explicit_text: The Governor of Minnesota, Rudy Perpich, honored my family farm in 1983 with an award recognizing over 100 years of service and contributions. This official recognition highlighted the importance of our farm not just locally but across the state and beyond. It was a proud moment for my family, especially for my great-grandpa Charles Draeger, who had maintained and modernized the farm through the years.
